articles.title: Amazon is already offering new OpenAI products on AWS
articles.description: A day after OpenAI got Microsoft to agree to end exclusive rights, AWS announced a slate of OpenAI model offerings, including a new agent service.
articles.author: Julie Bort

articles.title: Amazon launches an AI-powered audio Q&A experience on product pages
articles.description: Amazon's new "Join the chat" feature lets you ask questions about products and receive AI-powered audio responses.
articles.author: Lauren Forristal

articles.title: Match Group invests $100M in Sniffies, a cruising app for gay men
articles.description: The app is Match Group's newest attempt to get mobile users excited about online romance again.
articles.author: Lucas Ropek

articles.author: Julie Bort

articles.title: Paragon is not collaborating with Italian authorities probing spyware attacks, report says
articles.description: Despite promising to help determine what happened with the hacks targeting journalists and activists in Italy, Israeli American spyware maker Paragon
articles.author: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai

articles.title: US Supreme Court appears split over controversial use of ‘geofence’ search warrants
articles.description: The U.S. top court is expected to rule on whether to allow police to identify criminal suspects by dragnet searching the databases of tech giants.
articles.author: Zack Whittaker

articles.title: Australia forces Big Tech firms to pay for news or face a 2.25% tax

articles.author: Kate Park

articles.title: Lovable launches its vibe-coding app on iOS and Android
articles.description: The app allows developers to vibe code web apps and websites on the go.
articles.author: Sarah Perez

articles.title: Founder of Shark Tank-backed startup Scholly sues his acquirer Sallie Mae
articles.description: Chris Gray is suing his startup’s acquirer, Sallie Mae, for wrongful termination and alleging it's selling student data through a subsidiary. Sallie
articles.author: Dominic-Madori Davis, Sean O'Kane

articles.title: Apple introduces a cheaper option for App Store subscriptions
articles.description: Apple is adding a new subscription option that lets app developers offer lower monthly pricing in exchange for a 12-month commitment.
articles.author: Sarah Perez

articles.title: Snapchat brings AI-powered conversational advertising to its app
articles.description: Users will be able to chat with a brand's AI agent to do things like ask questions and get recommendations.
articles.author: Aisha Malik

articles.title: YouTube is testing an AI-powered search feature that shows guided answers
articles.description: YouTube is rolling out the new AI search feature to Premium subscribers in the U.S. on an opt-in basis.
articles.author: Ivan Mehta

articles.title: BCI startup Neurable looks to license its ‘mind-reading’ tech for consumer wearables
articles.description: The startup specializes in "non-invasive" "mind-reading" tech — a kind of neural data collection that, its CEO hopes, will have all sorts of consumer
articles.author: Lucas Ropek

articles.title: Red Hat’s OpenClaw maintainer just made enterprise Claw deployments a lot safer
articles.description: Tank OS puts OpenClaw AI agents into a container that lets it run reliably and more safely, especially for those running fleets of them.
articles.author: Julie Bort

articles.author: Ivan Mehta

articles.title: Drizzle on top: a new high-end dog food brand is coming for the 1%
articles.description: Golden Child is launching with two "five-star" products: a fresh frozen meal system and, more intriguingly, a "drizzle." It has also raised $37
articles.author: Connie Loizos

articles.title: India’s Snabbit closes $56M round as investor interest in on-demand home services heats up
articles.description: Snabbit now processes over 40,000 daily jobs and has cut costs sharply as it expands across cities and services.
articles.author: Jagmeet Singh
